title: Cheilanthes vestita
taxonomy: Cheilanthes vestita is classified under the family Pteridaceae, which is a family of ferns. It belongs to the genus Cheilanthes, a group of ferns commonly known as lip ferns or rock ferns. The species Cheilanthes vestita is further classified under the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse order of ferns. The kingdom to which Cheilanthes vestita belongs is Plantae, the plant kingdom. This classification helps in identifying and understanding the evolutionary relationships of the plant.

intro: Cheilanthes vestita, also known as the Silver Spleenwort, is a species of fern native to Australia, characterized by its delicate, silver-green fronds. Traditionally, Cheilanthes vestita has been used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cheilanthes vestita is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of tinctures and capsules to harness its health benefits.
